do_not_merge.reason: Test 1 (contradiction test) applies: entity_type differs — "department" vs "agency department" — which is a strong identity field. Per Identity Merge Canon, if any strong identity field differs the records are distinct regardless of name similarity. The first record also has documented part_of_agency relations to MTA and multiple source supports, while the second is isolated; merging would corrupt the graph.

suggestion.rationale: The two records share the same display name and entity_name, but the payload entity_type differs (department vs agency department). This is a strong identity field difference under the Identity Merge Canon, triggering test 1 (contradiction) and forcing a do_not_merge. No other evidence overrides this. The records may represent the same real-world department with an inconsistent type label, but per policy we cannot merge to clean a validation error.
